summary: McKenzie reports that his blog was hit by a massive spam attack within seconds of bringing it back online, receiving over 200 spam comment submissions about gambling. Most of the spam bypassed his existing spam filter. Rather than implementing intrusive anti-spam measures like registration requirements, he opted for a simple keyword ban on three gambling-related terms.
key_points: The blog received 200+ spam comments within 45 seconds of coming back online, suggesting automated bot monitoring
The existing spam filter failed badly, letting 180 of 200+ spam comments through
McKenzie chose the least intrusive solution — banning three specific words — over requiring user registration
money_quotes: Within 45 seconds of bringing my blog back from the dead I got over 200 spam comment submissions, mostly to old posts.

To prevent me getting repetitive stress disorder from hitting the 'mark as spam' button and keep this blog easy to comment on without requiring registration or anything intrusive, I've banned the following three words from comments: poker, casino, and gambling.
key_insight: When fighting spam, prefer the simplest targeted fix that preserves user experience over heavy-handed measures like mandatory registration.
